summary: PQ-Aliro presents a post-quantum adaptation of the Aliro NFC access-control protocol with useful engineering contributions. However, §7 is entirely empty (one sentence), making the formal proof claim unfulfilled. Several foundational issues in the security model further reduce confidence.
strengths: Byte-accurate APDU overhead analysis for all 9 parameter combinations
Android HCE key isolation technique (isolatedProcess for Dilithium private key)
Asynchronous signing to prevent ANR deadlocks
ML-KEM/ML-DSA parameter sizes consistent with FIPS 203/204
weaknesses: CRITICAL: §7 Security Proof contains only one sentence, no actual proof
MAJOR: Transcript hash trans=H(pk_e||c_e) does not bind long-term identities—identity-misbinding vulnerability
MAJOR: Blindness and strong one-wayness definitions in §4 are never used anywhere
MAJOR: Security level claim error—ML-KEM-768 is Level 3 (AES-192 ~96-bit quantum), not 128-bit post-quantum security
MAJOR: IND-CCA2 vs OW-PCA relationship never established; Fujisaki-Okamoto transform not cited
MODERATE: GCM IV construction non-standard, session key lifecycle not specified
MODERATE: Vestigial cyclic group notation (g, G, p) from earlier ECC draft, never used
MODERATE: Forward privacy definition conflates unlinkability with forward secrecy
MINOR: No repository URL for open-source implementation

detailed_comments.comment: §7 contains only: 'We prove that PQ-Aliro is secure in the Bellare-Rogaway-style AKE security model.' No proof whatsoever. Abstract and introduction list formal proof as primary contribution.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Provide complete proof or reframe as security argument with proof sketch

detailed_comments.comment: Transcript hash trans=H(pk_e||c_e) does not include long-term keys pk_R or pk_UD. Enables identity-misbinding attacks.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Include long-term public keys in transcript: trans=H(pk_e||c_e||pk_R||pk_UD) or at minimum include reader_identifier

detailed_comments.comment: Blindness (Def 4.1) and strong one-wayness (Def 4.3) introduced but never referenced again. Vestigial cyclic group generator g, prime order p never used.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Remove unused definitions or connect them to the proof; remove ECC-era notation

detailed_comments.comment: Paper states D3×K768 provides 128-bit post-quantum security. Incorrect: Level 3=AES-192=~96-bit quantum security. Level 1=AES-128=~64-bit quantum.
GCM IV=0x0000000000000001||ctr is non-standard; session key lifecycle undefined.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Clarify that SK_device is used for exactly one AEAD invocation, cite NIST SP 800-38D

detailed_comments.comment: HKDF construction not cited (RFC 5869 missing). SHAKE256 used but FIPS 202 not cited.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Add citations: RFC 5869, NIST SP 800-38D, FIPS 197, FIPS 202

detailed_comments.comment: Forward privacy experiment tests unlinkability, not forward secrecy. Paper uses 'perfect forward secrecy' which is overstated.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Replace 'perfect forward secrecy' with 'forward secrecy (under long-term key corruption)'

detailed_comments.comment: LaTeX label for §4 is \label{definition 1} rather than a proper section label.
Fujisaki-Okamoto transform not cited despite being foundation of ML-KEM IND-CCA2 security. CRYSTALS-Kyber/Dilithium round-3 submission documents not cited.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Add FO transform citation, Kyber/Dilithium round-3 submission docs

missing_citations.claim: ML-KEM IND-CCA2 security via FO transform
missing_citations.suggested_reference_type: Fujisaki-Okamoto CRYPTO 1999 / Journal of Cryptology 2013; Hofheinz-Hövelmanns-Kiltz TCC 2017
